risks: No Azure Communication Services resource included — Logic App references ACS SMS connector but the ACS resource is not provisioned in the plan
No monitoring or observability resources (Application Insights, Log Analytics) — limits operational visibility into the streaming pipeline
Single-region deployment (eastus) with no failover or geo-redundancy strategy — Cosmos DB serverless is single-region only and Storage uses Standard_LRS
No managed identity configuration for service-to-service authentication — relies on default key-based access between IoT Hub, Stream Analytics, Cosmos DB, and Service Bus
Public network access enabled on Cosmos DB and Container Registry — no private endpoint or VNet integration configured
Standard_LRS storage provides only local redundancy — IoT Hub file uploads and Stream Analytics checkpoint data have no zone or geo protection
correctionsRecommended: Add an Azure Communication Services (Microsoft.Communication/communicationServices) resource to enable the Logic App SMS connector
Add Application Insights and a Log Analytics workspace for end-to-end monitoring of IoT Hub, Stream Analytics, and Logic App workflows
Configure managed identities on Stream Analytics, Logic App, and IoT Hub for secure, keyless access to Cosmos DB, Service Bus, and Storage
Consider private endpoints or VNet service endpoints for Cosmos DB and Container Registry to reduce attack surface
Add resource tags (environment, project, cost-center) across all resources for governance and cost tracking
Consider upgrading Storage to Standard_ZRS for zone-level redundancy on checkpoint and staging data
